mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it ideal for machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing resources.

steps.title: Add KubeRay Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay easily.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the KubeRay repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Ray applications on Kubernetes.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay operator is running correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> You should see the KubeRay operator pod running.

steps.title: Create a RayCluster
steps.description:
This step creates a RayCluster custom resource, which is necessary to run Ray applications.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: my-ray-cluster
  namespace: kuberay
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a RayCluster with 1 head and 2 worker nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: my-ray-cluster
  namespace: kuberay
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify RayCluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RayCluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay

```

> You should see your RayCluster listed with a status of 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the KubeRay operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> This command removes the KubeRay operator from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades the KubeRay operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > rayclusters-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Backup existing RayCluster configurations.
> Upgrades the KubeRay operator.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > rayclusters-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RayCluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your Kubernetes cluster has enough resources allocated for the RayCluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeRay operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay operator may not be installed or is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' to check the status of the operator.

troubleshooting.title: RayCluster status shows 'Pending'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the required nodes are not available.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure they are ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Job failed with exit code 1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the job configuration is incorrect or resources are insufficient.

**Fix:**
Review the job configuration and logs for errors using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved resource management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RayCluster listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
